title: Electrical, ESD, Battery, Fire, Lifting, and Workspace Safety
description: A repair clinic has a swollen battery, a heavy printer, an energized power supply, loose jewelry, poor ventilation, and no documented emergency or disposal route. Learners cumulatively produce safe work authorization and hazard-control plan through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Survey electrical, thermal, chemical, mechanical, trip, ergonomic, fire, and data hazards before selecting controls.
Match grounding, antistatic bags, mats, humidity, handling, and packaging controls to static-sensitive work.
Recognize energized, capacitor, liquid, damaged-battery, odor, heat, and deformation stop conditions and route them to qualified handling.
